title: Website Project
tagline: Responsive personal portfolio built with HTML, CSS, and JavaScript.
summary: A first portfolio website that brought together hero, about, skills, projects, and contact sections in one clean, shareable flow.
description: This project began as a straightforward one-page portfolio and helped me practice how sections connect, how anchor navigation improves browsing, and how a complete site can be published online. Bringing it into this React portfolio keeps the original idea alive while presenting it with clearer structure and stronger case-study detail.
features: Single-page layout with hero, about, skills, projects, and contact sections
Anchor navigation that helps visitors move through the portfolio quickly
Projects area inspired by the original HTML, CSS, and JavaScript version
Responsive spacing and typography tuned for desktop and mobile viewing
metrics: Published on GitHub Pages for easy sharing and iteration
Strengthened confidence with HTML structure, CSS styling, and JavaScript setup
Created the foundation for later upgrades into React and Tailwind CSS

title: Logo Design
tagline: Beginner branding project focused on a simple startup identity concept.
summary: A design exercise centered on creating a memorable logo, pairing it with supporting colors, and presenting it through clean mockups.
description: The logo design project gave me space to think visually instead of only through code. It focused on clarity, balance, and first impressions by exploring logo marks, type pairings, and a small brand palette that could feel professional even in a simple presentation.
features: Explored several logo directions before refining one clear final concept
Built a small palette and type pairing to support the brand direction
Created presentation-style mockups to show the logo in realistic contexts
Practiced visual consistency across icon, wordmark, and support graphics
metrics: Improved confidence in composition, spacing, and color balance
Turned a basic startup idea into a more thoughtful visual identity direction
Expanded my portfolio beyond code into design communication

title: Java Backend Project
tagline: A practical banking system demonstrating core OOP principles and clean Java design.
summary: A foundational Java exercise that strengthened object-oriented thinking through a real-world banking system with account management, transactions, and error handling.
description: The Banking System project demonstrates how to build practical backend logic using Java. It focuses on core OOP concepts including encapsulation, proper error handling, and maintainable code structure. The system manages bank accounts with deposit, withdrawal, and transfer operations—showing how different objects interact in a cohesive system.
features: BankAccount class with private fields and controlled access (encapsulation)
Deposit method with validation for positive amounts
Withdraw method with balance checking and error handling
Clear feedback messages for each operation
Account information display with formatted output
Practical demonstration of state management and business logic
metrics: Simple, readable code focused on clarity over complexity
Proper error handling that prevents invalid operations
Real-world use case that connects abstract OOP to practical applications
Foundation for understanding how backend systems manage data and operations
